conjecture_title: Open Problem (Section 1.2): APX-hardness on classes without sublinear separators
paper_title: Thin graph classes and polynomial-time approximation schemes
summary: Dvořák asks whether maximum independent set is APX-hard on every subgraph-closed graph class that lacks sublinear separators (or at least has exponential expansion). No follow-up paper proving or disproving this was found after an exhaustive search. Related positive results (PTAS, QPTAS for bounded-expansion and sublinear-separator classes) confirm the structural boundary the problem targets, but the hardness side remains unresolved as of 2026.
search_queries: APX-hard maximum independent set subgraph-closed class sublinear separators

notes: No follow-up resolving the hardness question was found in 5 web calls. The conjecture sits at the boundary between tractability (PTAS on classes with strongly sublinear separators) and hardness (APX-hard on 3-regular graphs, which have no sublinear separators); the intermediate regime—subgraph-closed classes with exponential expansion but no 3-regular graphs—appears untouched in the indexed literature.
